task_completion.description: Open the patient record for John Zenon in GNU Health.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GNU Health interface showing 'John Zenon' as the active patient in the patient view or search results.

task_completion.description: Document the toxic inhalation diagnosis using an appropriate ICD-10 code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A condition/diagnosis entry for John Zenon showing an ICD-10 code starting with J68 or T59 (e.g., J68.x, T59.x).

task_completion.description: Perform a clinical evaluation documenting severe respiratory distress.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Clinical evaluation or vital signs form showing a Respiratory Rate of 24 or higher AND an Oxygen Saturation (O2 Sat) of 94% or lower.
Medication prescription or order screen showing one of the approved rescue medications (Albuterol, Salbutamol, Prednisone, Dexamethasone, Budesonide, or Ipratropium) for John Zenon.

task_completion.description: Order at least two baseline diagnostic laboratory tests.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Laboratory test order screen showing at least two different lab tests ordered for the patient.

task_completion.description: Schedule a pulmonary follow-up appointment in 7 to 14 days.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Appointment scheduling screen showing an appointment booked for John Zenon with a date that is 7 to 14 days in the future.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GNU Health graphical user interface rather than using terminal commands or scripts to modify the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GNU Health desktop client being actively used, with forms being filled out.

integrity.description: The agent actually entered the data into the application forms rather than just typing the requirements into a text editor or terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data is visible inside the appropriate GNU Health fields (e.g., patient search, vital signs, prescriptions, lab orders).

integrity.description: The agent did not open a terminal to run SQL commands to insert the records directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows showing SQL INSERT or UPDATE commands related to the patient's records.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient Name: John Zenon. Diagnosis must use an ICD-10 code starting with J68 or T59. Clinical evaluation must show Respiratory Rate >= 24 and Oxygen Saturation <= 94%. Valid rescue medications include Albuterol, Salbutamol, Prednisone, Dexamethasone, Budesonide, or Ipratropium. At least 2 lab tests must be ordered. Follow-up appointment must be scheduled 7 to 14 days from the current date.
